sections.paragraph:
an unused root, Arab. أَابَ for أَوَبَ is-

(1) to return, also to come to one’s senses, whence أَواَّبُ resipiscens.

(2) to set, as the sun.

(3) to come by night, especially to seek for water. Conj. V, VIII, id., أَيَّابُ (not أَبَّابُ which misprint of the first edition of my Lexicon, has been copied by Winer); a water-bearer (Kam. p. 46; not uter, a bottle, as in Golius). In Hebrew, hence-
